concerns.text: Phase 6 (Reviewer Revision) tasks T036–T039 introduce a 'Tractography Confidence Sensitivity' analysis that has no basis in spec.md (FR-006/FR-008 only mandate window length and density threshold). This constitutes unapproved scope creep. The task order implies a dependency on a non-existent requirement, violating the 'Producer before consumer' principle where the spec (producer) must define the artifact (consumer) before implementation tasks are ordered.

Task T017 (LOO K-Means) is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, the algorithm requires iterating through subjects and excluding the target subject 'i' from the cohort 'j != i' to derive centroids. This implies a shared state (the full cohort dataset) that must be read and filtered for every iteration. If 'parallel-safe' implies running T017 for multiple subjects simultaneously without locking or data duplication, it risks race conditions or incorrect centroid derivation if the exclusion logic isn't strictly isolated per process. The [P] tag is potentially unsafe without explicit isolation guarantees.

Task T016 describes 'sliding-window correlation' generically. The spec (FR-002) mandates specific parameters (30 TR window, 1 TR step, concatenation across subjects). While not a hard ordering violation, the lack of specific parameters in the task description creates a risk that the 'producer' (T016) generates an artifact incompatible with the 'consumer' (T017/T025) which expects the specific concatenated matrix structure defined in the spec.
